Let $S=\{2,3,4, \ldots\}$ denote the set of integers that are greater than or equal to $2$. Does there exist a function $f: S \rightarrow S$ such that
$$
f(a) f(b) = f\left(a^{2} b^{2}\right) \text{ for all } a, b \in S \text{ with } a \neq b?
$$

Consider a=2,b=6: f(2)f(6)=f(144).
Consider a=3,b=4: f(3)f(4)=f(144). So f(2)f(6)=f(3)f(4).
Consider a=2,b=12: f(2)f(12)=f(576).
Consider a=3,b=8: f(3)f(8)=f(576).
Consider a=4,b=6: f(4)f(6)=f(576). So f(2)f(12)=f(3)f(8)=f(4)f(6).

Also a=6,b=4 gives f(4)f(6)=f(576), same.

Now: f(2)f(6)=f(3)f(4) (from 144), and f(4)f(6)=f(2)f(12) (from 576). Divide: f(2)/f(4)=f(3)f(4)/(f(2)f(6)) ... let me just set variables.

Let p=f(2), q=f(3), r=f(4), s=f(6), t=f(8), u=f(9), v=f(12).

ps = qr (from 144)
pv = qt = rs (from 576)

From pv=rs: v=rs
